description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.title: SEC-5.14/5.15/5.16: Sections exist in HTML but are entirely absent from DOCX/PDF build
issues.description: SEC-5.14 (SEC-5.14.json) + SEC-5.15 (SEC-5.15.json) + SEC-5.16 (SEC-5.16.json): Sections 5.14, 5.15, and 5.16 exist in generate-html.js but are entirely absent from build-doc-v4.js. The DOCX/PDF skips from 5.13 to Part 6. These sections include some of the review's strongest original arguments about dome author's own AI-context engineering. Must add to DOCX builder. Third curmudgeon flag on this gap. Source: SEC-5.14/5.15/5.16 reviews.
issues.notes: Reassigned from analyst per proposal-ISS-808-reassign.json: EXP-122 is build infrastructure (build-doc-v4.js changes), not scientific analysis. Analyst does not own this file. Requires tinker or human to add SEC-5.14/5.15/5.16 to the DOCX builder.

issues.description: External report #1 (GitHub): Positive feedback from reader (Devilwench). No scientific error claimed. No action required.
issues.wontfix_reason: Not a problem report — reader expressing support. No kernel of truth or correction to evaluate.

issues.description: Evaluation Guide eg-verdicts section (in generate-html.js): count-inflation preemption paragraph uses "(045–051)" range which spans SEVEN WIN IDs but only five tidal WINs exist (045, 046, 049, 050, 051). Also: logical overlap in false-dilemma subtype list item (3) should be dropped. Cannot be self-applied (generate-html.js is infrastructure). Needs human patch.

Evaluation Guide eg-falsifiability section (in generate-html.js): WIN-044 mischaracterized as "produces value that matches standard physics or contradicts measurement" — WIN-044 actually has verdict Self-Contradicted (dome derives a value that conflicts with its own other equations), not a standard-physics or measurement contradiction. Also "only 2 of 69 claims even attempt a dome-geometry derivation" is contestable — "attempt" is ambiguous. Cannot be self-applied (generate-html.js is infrastructure).
SEC-4-intro: verdict enumeration omits "Misleading" (22/70, the largest category). Current text lists only 4 of 6 verdict types. Patched in this run.
issues.fix_details: Patched in decider run 2026-04-14T15:28 — added Misleading/Std-Model-Explains/Self-Contradicted/etc. enumeration.

issues.description: WIN-010 detail_verdict_text: typo in H(r) formula — "exp(−1r/8619)" should be "exp(−r/8619)". Stray leading 1 in the exponent.
issues.fix_details: Patched in decider run 2026-04-14T15:28 — removed stray 1 from exponent.

issues.description: WIN-010 detail_verdict_text: "Meza et al. (2021, JGR Space Physics)" citation unverified — Meza eclipse papers typically appear in JASTP, not JGR Space Physics. Needs end-to-end verification (full authors, year, journal, DOI) or replacement with a verifiable citation.

SEC-6.5-FAIL004: defense_survives=3. (1) AAO proxy for 500hPa prediction: section critiques dome for mixing metrics but uses 700hPa as proxy for 500hPa prediction — needs either defense of the proxy or correction. (2) "Retroactively labeled refined" needs primary source citation (dome_label in failures.json is internal, not public-facing citation).

WIN-058b: r=0.96 vs r=0.992 correlation inconsistency has an unaddressed defender escape. The dome could argue these represent different data windows, calibration epochs, or baseline-subtraction choices. Section should either (a) verify both r-values from same methodology or (b) use only the lower r-value and acknowledge the range.
issues.fix_details: Text these issues describe (thermospheric claim, r=0.96) no longer exists in WIN-058b after EXP-102 rewrite. Issues are stale.
issues.staleness_note: Re-opened: r=0.96 IS present in detail_verdict_text; the text reads: The claimed prospective prediction cites r = 0.96 while the same home page cites r = 0.992. Cannot verify source of r=0.96 in dome raw text - needs yeet to analyst.

issues.description: SEC-7.1 Antarctic paragraph cites Soviet Ob (1956-57), HMS Endurance, and modern Southern Ocean yacht races as confirming ~13,700 km figure via 70°S circumnavigation. None of these actually performed full 70°S circumnavigations: Soviet Ob did coastal work for Mirny resupply; HMS Endurance patrols South Georgia/Peninsula sector; modern yacht races (Vendée Globe, Volvo, Jules Verne) run at 40-55°S due to sea ice. The actual basis for the 13,700 km figure is coastline cartography + satellite altimetry, NOT vessel circumnavigation. Recommended fix: replace ship citations with SCAR ADD / IBCSO / Bellingshausen 1819-21 (canonical first Antarctic circumnavigation, at higher latitudes/non-continuous 70°S track). Out-of-scope from EXP-161 which limited itself to v22 quote and AE projection.
issues.notes: Filed by human after noticing decider report flagged this for follow-up but never created an issue. The pipeline gap: "out of scope, tracked for follow-up" comments in decider reports do not actually create tracked work items.

issues.description: Part 4 and Part 5 each contain one inline italic transition paragraph using duplicated styling (font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-bottom:0.8em). Curmudgeon flagged in part4-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #5 and part5-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #4 as out-of-scope for those EXPs but recommended consolidation. Recommended fix: migrate both transitions to a shared CSS class (e.g., .section-transition) defined in the stylesheet to ease global theme changes. Status check confirms styles still inline as of 2026-04-14.
issues.notes: Filed during human-requested audit of old reports for deferred-but-untracked work. The two curmudgeon reviews flagged this as out-of-scope for their respective EXPs but never resulted in a tracked work item.
